/* ============================================

:root {

}

/* ============================================
   RESET & BASE
   ============================================ */
*, *::before, *::after { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }

html { scroll-behavior: smooth; }

body {
  font-family: var(--font-body);
  background: var(--bg);
  color: var(--text);
  line-height: 1.6;

}

a { color: inherit; text-decoration: none; }
img { max-width: 100%; display: block; }

/* ============================================

.container {
  max-width: 1200px;
  margin: 0 auto;
  padding: 0 24px;
}

section {
  padding: 120px 0;
}

/* ============================================

   ============================================ */
nav {
  position: fixed;
  top: 0;
  left: 0;
  right: 0;
  z-index: 100;
  padding: 20px 0;
  background: rgba(250,250,248,0.92);
  backdrop-filter: blur(20px);
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(28,27,26,0.08);
}

nav .container {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: space-between;
}

.logo {
  font-family: var(--font-display);
  font-size: 18px;
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: -0.5px;
}



.nav-links {
  display: flex;
  gap: 32px;
  align-items: center;
  list-style: none;
}

.nav-links a {
  font-size: 14px;
  font-weight: 400;
  color: var(--gray);
  transition: color 0.2s;
}

.nav-links a:hover { color: var(--text); }

.nav-cta {
  background: var(--accent);
  color: #ffffff !important;
  padding: 10px 24px;
  border-radius: 6px;
  font-weight: 600;
  font-size: 14px;
  transition: background 0.2s;
}

.nav-cta:hover { background: var(--accent-glow) !important; }

We don't select founders from applications. The best people in our evenings get invited to hackathons. The best hackathon teams get first look at Residency spots. Merit shows up in code, not cover letters.

No equity. Ever.

Accelerators take 7%. We take nothing. Our sponsors fund these events because they want deal flow — which means our incentives are perfectly aligned with yours.

Stories

From the people who shipped.

"Was building alone in Leuven. Week 5 I had a team and users."

Leon Dehullu
Founder, Stealth startup

"Came for the vibe. Stayed because I actually launched and found people who ship."

Vincent Demarez
Founder, Friday
FAQ

Got questions?

General
What is Habitat?
Habitat runs evening sprints, technical hackathons, and a founder residency across Belgium. You show up with an idea, and you leave with something real — a working prototype, a landing page, your first users. We take no equity, ever. Our programs are free, funded by sponsors who want access to the best builders in Europe.
Do I need to be technical to join?
Not at all. Many of our best MVPs have been built by non-technical founders using AI tools like Cursor, Lovable, and v0, or no-code platforms. The framework works for everyone — what matters is that you want to build something.
Where do events happen?
Currently in four Belgian cities: Leuven, Ghent, Brussels, and Antwerp. We're expanding to more cities soon. All upcoming events are listed on our Luma calendar.
Is there an application process?
For Evenings — no application. You register on Luma and show up. For Hackathons — we select for technical ability and drive. For the Residency — yes, it's selective. You need to have shipped an MVP already. The philosophy is simple: you rise by building, not by applying.
Do you really take no equity?
Zero. Ever. Accelerators typically take 5–7%. We take nothing. Our sponsors fund the events because they want access to deal flow — meaning our incentives are perfectly aligned with yours. You keep everything you build.
How is Habitat different from a hackathon?
Traditional hackathons are 24–48 hours, team-based, and focused on a technical challenge. Habitat Evenings are 3–4 hours, individual-focused, and the output is a business — landing page, prototype, first users — not a demo project. That said, we also run our own Technical Hackathons for deeper builds.
How is this different from Startup Weekend?
Startup Weekend is 54 hours over a full weekend with pitching, team formation, and judges. Habitat Evenings are one evening — no pitching (until the optional 60-second demo at the end), no formal team formation (you build your own thing), and no judges.
Habitat Evenings
What happens at a Habitat Evening?
You arrive with an idea. Over 3–4 hours, you go through a structured sprint: define your problem, build a landing page, create a prototype, and get it in front of real people. At the end, there are optional 60-second lightning demos. Then you go home with something live.
Do I need to come with an idea?
Ideally, yes. The registration asks "What idea are you thinking of building?" If you don't have one, you can join someone's table and contribute to their build. But the best outcomes come from people who arrive with something they want to create.
How much does it cost?
Evenings are free. Food and drinks are provided. Everything is funded by our sponsors.
How many people attend?
Around 40 builders per evening. We cap registration to keep things focused and high-energy. Events tend to sell out within 48 hours, so join the waitlist to get notified early.
Can companies send their teams?
Yes. We also run private Habitat sprints for corporate teams. Contact us for our B2B workshop offering.

Sponsors fund our events in exchange for access to deal flow — the builders and startups that come out of our programs. Sponsors cannot present, pitch, or take the stage during events. Habitat remains the primary brand. Get in touch if you're interested.

Can I host Habitat at my venue or coworking space?

content.text: Now add the hamburger JS to index.html:

input.old_string:
<script>
const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
  entries.forEach(entry => {
    if (entry.isIntersecting) {
      entry.target.classList.add('visible');
    }
  });
}, { threshold: 0.1 });

document.querySelectorAll('.fade-up').forEach(el => observer.observe(el));
</script>

</body>
</html>
input.new_string:
<script>
const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
  entries.forEach(entry => {
    if (entry.isIntersecting) {
      entry.target.classList.add('visible');
    }
  });
}, { threshold: 0.1 });

document.querySelectorAll('.fade-up').forEach(el => observer.observe(el));

const hamburger = document.getElementById('hamburger');
const navLinks = document.getElementById('nav-links');
hamburger.addEventListener('click', () => {
  hamburger.classList.toggle('open');
  navLinks.classList.toggle('open');
});
navLinks.querySelectorAll('a').forEach(link => {
  link.addEventListener('click', () => {
    hamburger.classList.remove('open');
    navLinks.classList.remove('open');
  });
});
</script>

</body>
</html>

[Habitat]
  • Events
  • Hackathons
  • Residency
  • Why Habitat
  • Join waitlist →
Technical Hackathons

One day. One team.
One working product.

Full-day deep builds for developers and technical founders. Real infrastructure. Real stakes. Ship or learn — either way you win.

Duration 10am – 8pm
Team size 2-3 people
Output Working demo
Register interest → See the format
The format

10 hours of focused building.

No slides. No pitch decks at the start. You arrive, you form a team, you build. At the end, you demo a working product to the judges and your peers.

10:00

Kickoff & theme reveal

The technical theme or problem space is announced. Team up, pick your angle, define your scope. You have 30 minutes to align — then you build.

10:30

Building begins

Heads down. Real code, real infrastructure, real product. Experienced mentors are in the room to unblock you when you're stuck.

13:00

Lunch + mid-day check-in

Break, eat, recalibrate. Optional 5-minute mid-day check-ins with a mentor if you've hit a wall or want to pressure-test your approach.

14:00

Back to building

Afternoon block. Focus on making it work end-to-end, not just look good. A working demo matters more than a polished UI.

18:00

Freeze + prep demos

Code freeze. Two hours to prep your demo — no new features, just make it presentable and tell the story clearly.

19:00

Demo + pitches

Each team gets 5 minutes. Live demo, one slide max. Mentors and peers ask real questions. The best teams get follow-up conversations, not just a prize.

20:00

Awards + networking

Prizes, credits, and introductions. More importantly: the people in this room are who you want to know.

Who attends

Built for people who ship.

This isn't a general hackathon. We select for technical ability and the drive to build real things — not to win competitions.

⚡

Technical founders

You've already shipped something. You build fast. You want a focused day with peers at your level and feedback from people who get technical products.

🛠

Developers exploring startups

You're a strong engineer who's been thinking about building your own thing. This is a low-stakes way to test if you like the founder experience.

🎓

CS & engineering students

From KU Leuven, UGent, VUB, or elsewhere. You want to build something real and meet people who are doing it.

What you walk away with

More than a weekend project.

The goal isn't to win a hackathon. The goal is to leave with something worth continuing — and people worth building with.

A working technical demo

Not a mockup. Not a pitch deck. A live product that does something real — and that you built under pressure in 8 hours.

Meaningful introductions

Experienced founders and builders are in the room all day. If your build gets their attention, the conversation starts that evening — not 6 months later.

Your co-founder, maybe

Teams form on the day. Some of the best founder partnerships start at events like this. You won't know until you build together.

FAQ

Got questions?

Technical Hackathons
What are Technical Hackathons?
Full-day deep builds (10am–8pm) for developers and technical founders. You form a team of 2–3, build a working product around a revealed theme, and demo it to everyone at the end of the day. Think of it as the next level up from our Evenings.
Do I need a team beforehand?
No. Teams form on the day after the theme is revealed. Some of the best founder partnerships start at events like this. You won't know until you build together.
Who are the judges?
Experienced founders and builders are in the room all day — mentoring during the build and judging the demos. The best teams get follow-up conversations, not just a prize.
What do I need to bring?
Your laptop, your skills, and the drive to ship something real. We provide the venue, food, mentors, and infrastructure.
When is the first hackathon?
Coming soon. Register your interest and we'll notify you when the first one opens.

Ready to build?

Register your interest and we'll notify you when the next technical hackathon opens.

Register interest → Back to Habitat
[Habitat]

The fastest path from zero to real. Building across Belgium since 2025.

Programs

Evenings Technical Hackathons Residency

Community

Events LinkedIn

Contact

Apply / Partner
© 2026 Habitat. Made with care in Belgium. Leuven · Ghent · Brussels · Antwerp

[Habitat]
  • Events

  • Join waitlist →
Technical Hackathons

One day. One team.
One working product.

Full-day deep builds for developers and technical founders. Real infrastructure. Real stakes. Ship or learn — either way you win.

Duration 10am – 8pm
Team size 2-3 people
Output Working demo
Register interest → See the format
The format

10 hours of focused building.

No slides. No pitch decks at the start. You arrive, you form a team, you build. At the end, you demo a working product to the judges and your peers.

10:00

Kickoff & theme reveal

The technical theme or problem space is announced. Team up, pick your angle, define your scope. You have 30 minutes to align — then you build.

10:30

Building begins

Heads down. Real code, real infrastructure, real product. Experienced mentors are in the room to unblock you when you're stuck.

13:00

Lunch + mid-day check-in

Break, eat, recalibrate. Optional 5-minute mid-day check-ins with a mentor if you've hit a wall or want to pressure-test your approach.

14:00

Back to building

Afternoon block. Focus on making it work end-to-end, not just look good. A working demo matters more than a polished UI.

18:00

Freeze + prep demos

Code freeze. Two hours to prep your demo — no new features, just make it presentable and tell the story clearly.

19:00

Demo + pitches

Each team gets 5 minutes. Live demo, one slide max. Mentors and peers ask real questions. The best teams get follow-up conversations, not just a prize.

20:00

Awards + networking

Prizes, credits, and introductions. More importantly: the people in this room are who you want to know.

Who attends

Built for people who ship.

This isn't a general hackathon. We select for technical ability and the drive to build real things — not to win competitions.

⚡

Technical founders

You've already shipped something. You build fast. You want a focused day with peers at your level and feedback from people who get technical products.

🛠

Developers exploring startups

You're a strong engineer who's been thinking about building your own thing. This is a low-stakes way to test if you like the founder experience.

🎓

CS & engineering students

From KU Leuven, UGent, VUB, or elsewhere. You want to build something real and meet people who are doing it.

What you walk away with

More than a weekend project.

The goal isn't to win a hackathon. The goal is to leave with something worth continuing — and people worth building with.

A working technical demo

Not a mockup. Not a pitch deck. A live product that does something real — and that you built under pressure in 8 hours.

Meaningful introductions

Experienced founders and builders are in the room all day. If your build gets their attention, the conversation starts that evening — not 6 months later.

Your co-founder, maybe

Teams form on the day. Some of the best founder partnerships start at events like this. You won't know until you build together.

FAQ

Got questions?

Technical Hackathons
What are Technical Hackathons?
Full-day deep builds (10am–8pm) for developers and technical founders. You form a team of 2–3, build a working product around a revealed theme, and demo it to everyone at the end of the day. Think of it as the next level up from our Evenings.
Do I need a team beforehand?
No. Teams form on the day after the theme is revealed. Some of the best founder partnerships start at events like this. You won't know until you build together.
Who are the judges?
Experienced founders and builders are in the room all day — mentoring during the build and judging the demos. The best teams get follow-up conversations, not just a prize.
What do I need to bring?
Your laptop, your skills, and the drive to ship something real. We provide the venue, food, mentors, and infrastructure.
When is the first hackathon?
Coming soon. Register your interest and we'll notify you when the first one opens.

Ready to build?

Register your interest and we'll notify you when the next technical hackathon opens.

Why Habitat

Built different.
No equity. No noise.

Most programs pick you. We watch you build. Start with an evening. Go deeper when you're ready.

FAQ

Got questions?

General
What is Habitat?
Habitat runs evening sprints, technical hackathons, and a founder residency across Belgium. You show up with an idea, and you leave with something real — a working prototype, a landing page, your first users. We take no equity, ever. Our programs are free, funded by sponsors who want access to the best builders in Europe.
Do I need to be technical to join?
Not at all. Many of our best MVPs have been built by non-technical founders using AI tools like Cursor, Lovable, and v0, or no-code platforms. The framework works for everyone — what matters is that you want to build something.
Where do events happen?
Currently in four Belgian cities: Leuven, Ghent, Brussels, and Antwerp. We're expanding to more cities soon. All upcoming events are listed on our Luma calendar.
Is there an application process?
For Evenings — no application. You register on Luma and show up. For Hackathons — we select for technical ability and drive. For the Residency — yes, it's selective. You need to have shipped an MVP already. The philosophy is simple: you rise by building, not by applying.
Do you really take no equity?
Zero. Ever. Accelerators typically take 5–7%. We take nothing. Our sponsors fund the events because they want access to deal flow — meaning our incentives are perfectly aligned with yours. You keep everything you build.
How is Habitat different from a hackathon?
Traditional hackathons are 24–48 hours, team-based, and focused on a technical challenge. Habitat Evenings are 3–4 hours, individual-focused, and the output is a business — landing page, prototype, first users — not a demo project. That said, we also run our own Technical Hackathons for deeper builds.
How is this different from Startup Weekend?
Startup Weekend is 54 hours over a full weekend with pitching, team formation, and judges. Habitat Evenings are one evening — no pitching (until the optional 60-second demo at the end), no formal team formation (you build your own thing), and no judges.
Habitat Evenings
What happens at a Habitat Evening?
You arrive with an idea. Over 3–4 hours, you go through a structured sprint: define your problem, build a landing page, create a prototype, and get it in front of real people. At the end, there are optional 60-second lightning demos. Then you go home with something live.
Do I need to come with an idea?
Ideally, yes. The registration asks "What idea are you thinking of building?" If you don't have one, you can join someone's table and contribute to their build. But the best outcomes come from people who arrive with something they want to create.
How much does it cost?
Evenings are free. Food and drinks are provided. Everything is funded by our sponsors.
How many people attend?
Around 40 builders per evening. We cap registration to keep things focused and high-energy. Events tend to sell out within 48 hours, so join the waitlist to get notified early.
Can companies send their teams?
Yes. We also run private Habitat sprints for corporate teams. Contact us for our B2B workshop offering.
Partners & Sponsors
How does sponsorship work?
Sponsors fund our events in exchange for access to deal flow — the builders and startups that come out of our programs. Sponsors cannot present, pitch, or take the stage during events. Habitat remains the primary brand. Get in touch if you're interested.
Can I bring Habitat to my city?
Yes — if you're outside our current cities and want to run a Habitat evening, reach out. We'll walk you through the framework, share the playbook, and help you find a venue. We're actively looking for city leads across Europe.
Can I host Habitat at my venue or coworking space?
Absolutely. We're always looking for great venues. Ideal spaces seat 40+ people at tables (not theater-style), have good Wi-Fi, and allow for food and drinks. Contact us and we'll figure it out together.

Register interest → See the format
The format

10 hours of focused building.

No slides. No pitch decks at the start. You arrive, you form a team, you build. At the end, you demo a working product to the judges and your peers.

10:00

Kickoff & theme reveal

The technical theme or problem space is announced. Team up, pick your angle, define your scope. You have 30 minutes to align — then you build.

10:30

Building begins

Heads down. Real code, real infrastructure, real product. Experienced mentors are in the room to unblock you when you're stuck.

13:00

Lunch + mid-day check-in

Break, eat, recalibrate. Optional 5-minute mid-day check-ins with a mentor if you've hit a wall or want to pressure-test your approach.

14:00

Back to building

Afternoon block. Focus on making it work end-to-end, not just look good. A working demo matters more than a polished UI.

18:00

Freeze + prep demos

Code freeze. Two hours to prep your demo — no new features, just make it presentable and tell the story clearly.

19:00

Demo + pitches

Each team gets 5 minutes. Live demo, one slide max. Mentors and peers ask real questions. The best teams get follow-up conversations, not just a prize.

20:00

Awards + networking

Prizes, credits, and introductions. More importantly: the people in this room are who you want to know.

Who attends

Built for people who ship.

This isn't a general hackathon. We select for technical ability and the drive to build real things — not to win competitions.

⚡

Technical founders

You've already shipped something. You build fast. You want a focused day with peers at your level and feedback from people who get technical products.

🛠

Developers exploring startups

You're a strong engineer who's been thinking about building your own thing. This is a low-stakes way to test if you like the founder experience.

🎓

CS & engineering students

From KU Leuven, UGent, VUB, or elsewhere. You want to build something real and meet people who are doing it.

What you walk away with

More than a weekend project.

The goal isn't to win a hackathon. The goal is to leave with something worth continuing — and people worth building with.

A working technical demo

Not a mockup. Not a pitch deck. A live product that does something real — and that you built under pressure in 8 hours.

Meaningful introductions

Experienced founders and builders are in the room all day. If your build gets their attention, the conversation starts that evening — not 6 months later.

Your co-founder, maybe

Teams form on the day. Some of the best founder partnerships start at events like this. You won't know until you build together.

FAQ

Got questions?

Technical Hackathons
What are Technical Hackathons?
Full-day deep builds (10am–8pm) for developers and technical founders. You form a team of 2–3, build a working product around a revealed theme, and demo it to everyone at the end of the day. Think of it as the next level up from our Evenings.
Do I need a team beforehand?
No. Teams form on the day after the theme is revealed. Some of the best founder partnerships start at events like this. You won't know until you build together.
Who are the judges?
Experienced founders and builders are in the room all day — mentoring during the build and judging the demos. The best teams get follow-up conversations, not just a prize.
What do I need to bring?
Your laptop, your skills, and the drive to ship something real. We provide the venue, food, mentors, and infrastructure.
When is the first hackathon?
Coming soon. Register your interest and we'll notify you when the first one opens.

Why Habitat

Built different.
No equity. No noise.

Most programs pick you. We watch you build. Start with an evening. Go deeper when you're ready.

Test before you commit

Most startup advice skips the hard part: you don't know if an idea is worth six months until you've shown it to real people. Habitat lets you validate in an evening before deciding whether to go deeper.

FAQ

Got questions?

General
What is Habitat?
Habitat runs evening sprints, technical hackathons, and a founder residency across Belgium. You show up with an idea, and you leave with something real — a working prototype, a landing page, your first users. We take no equity, ever. Our programs are free, funded by sponsors who want access to the best builders in Europe.
Do I need to be technical to join?
Not at all. Many of our best MVPs have been built by non-technical founders using AI tools like Cursor, Lovable, and v0, or no-code platforms. The framework works for everyone — what matters is that you want to build something.
Where do events happen?
Currently in four Belgian cities: Leuven, Ghent, Brussels, and Antwerp. We're expanding to more cities soon. All upcoming events are listed on our Luma calendar.
Is there an application process?
For Evenings — no application. You register on Luma and show up. For Hackathons — we select for technical ability and drive. For the Residency — yes, it's selective. You need to have shipped an MVP already. The philosophy is simple: you rise by building, not by applying.
Do you really take no equity?
Zero. Ever. Accelerators typically take 5–7%. We take nothing. Our sponsors fund the events because they want access to deal flow — meaning our incentives are perfectly aligned with yours. You keep everything you build.
How is Habitat different from a hackathon?
Traditional hackathons are 24–48 hours, team-based, and focused on a technical challenge. Habitat Evenings are 3–4 hours, individual-focused, and the output is a business — landing page, prototype, first users — not a demo project. That said, we also run our own Technical Hackathons for deeper builds.
How is this different from Startup Weekend?
Startup Weekend is 54 hours over a full weekend with pitching, team formation, and judges. Habitat Evenings are one evening — no pitching (until the optional 60-second demo at the end), no formal team formation (you build your own thing), and no judges.
Habitat Evenings
What happens at a Habitat Evening?
You arrive with an idea. Over 3–4 hours, you go through a structured sprint: define your problem, build a landing page, create a prototype, and get it in front of real people. At the end, there are optional 60-second lightning demos. Then you go home with something live.
Do I need to come with an idea?
Ideally, yes. The registration asks "What idea are you thinking of building?" If you don't have one, you can join someone's table and contribute to their build. But the best outcomes come from people who arrive with something they want to create.
How much does it cost?
Evenings are free. Food and drinks are provided. Everything is funded by our sponsors.
How many people attend?
Around 40 builders per evening. We cap registration to keep things focused and high-energy. Events tend to sell out within 48 hours, so join the waitlist to get notified early.
Can companies send their teams?
Yes. We also run private Habitat sprints for corporate teams. Contact us for our B2B workshop offering.
Partners & Sponsors
How does sponsorship work?
Sponsors fund our events in exchange for access to deal flow — the builders and startups that come out of our programs. Sponsors cannot present, pitch, or take the stage during events. Habitat remains the primary brand. Get in touch if you're interested.
Can I bring Habitat to my city?
Yes — if you're outside our current cities and want to run a Habitat evening, reach out. We'll walk you through the framework, share the playbook, and help you find a venue. We're actively looking for city leads across Europe.
Can I host Habitat at my venue or coworking space?
Absolutely. We're always looking for great venues. Ideal spaces seat 40+ people at tables (not theater-style), have good Wi-Fi, and allow for food and drinks. Contact us and we'll figure it out together.

Technical Hackathons

One day. One team.
One working product.

Full-day deep builds for developers and technical founders. Real infrastructure. Real stakes. Ship or learn — either way you win.

Duration 10am – 8pm
Team size 2-3 people
Output Working demo
Register interest → See the format
The format

10 hours of focused building.

No slides. No pitch decks at the start. You arrive, you form a team, you build. At the end, you demo a working product to the judges and your peers.

10:00

Kickoff & theme reveal

The technical theme or problem space is announced. Team up, pick your angle, define your scope. You have 30 minutes to align — then you build.

10:30

Building begins

Heads down. Real code, real infrastructure, real product. Experienced mentors are in the room to unblock you when you're stuck.

13:00

Lunch + mid-day check-in

Break, eat, recalibrate. Optional 5-minute mid-day check-ins with a mentor if you've hit a wall or want to pressure-test your approach.

14:00

Back to building

Afternoon block. Focus on making it work end-to-end, not just look good. A working demo matters more than a polished UI.

18:00

Freeze + prep demos

Code freeze. Two hours to prep your demo — no new features, just make it presentable and tell the story clearly.

19:00

Demo + pitches

Each team gets 5 minutes. Live demo, one slide max. Mentors and peers ask real questions. The best teams get follow-up conversations, not just a prize.

20:00

Awards + networking

Prizes, credits, and introductions. More importantly: the people in this room are who you want to know.

Who attends

Built for people who ship.

This isn't a general hackathon. We select for technical ability and the drive to build real things — not to win competitions.

⚡

Technical founders

You've already shipped something. You build fast. You want a focused day with peers at your level and feedback from people who get technical products.

🛠

Developers exploring startups

You're a strong engineer who's been thinking about building your own thing. This is a low-stakes way to test if you like the founder experience.

🎓

CS & engineering students

From KU Leuven, UGent, VUB, or elsewhere. You want to build something real and meet people who are doing it.

What you walk away with

More than a weekend project.

The goal isn't to win a hackathon. The goal is to leave with something worth continuing — and people worth building with.

A working technical demo

Not a mockup. Not a pitch deck. A live product that does something real — and that you built under pressure in 8 hours.

Meaningful introductions

Experienced founders and builders are in the room all day. If your build gets their attention, the conversation starts that evening — not 6 months later.

Your co-founder, maybe

Teams form on the day. Some of the best founder partnerships start at events like this. You won't know until you build together.

FAQ

Got questions?

Technical Hackathons
What are Technical Hackathons?
Full-day deep builds (10am–8pm) for developers and technical founders. You form a team of 2–3, build a working product around a revealed theme, and demo it to everyone at the end of the day. Think of it as the next level up from our Evenings.
Do I need a team beforehand?
No. Teams form on the day after the theme is revealed. Some of the best founder partnerships start at events like this. You won't know until you build together.
Who are the judges?
Experienced founders and builders are in the room all day — mentoring during the build and judging the demos. The best teams get follow-up conversations, not just a prize.
What do I need to bring?
Your laptop, your skills, and the drive to ship something real. We provide the venue, food, mentors, and infrastructure.
When is the first hackathon?
Coming soon. Register your interest and we'll notify you when the first one opens.

Ready to build?

Register your interest and we'll notify you when the next technical hackathon opens.
